rationale: The GT's headline concern (Gaohan123: is source_outputs[0] really the parent? add a check/comment) is exactly what Y flags at hunyuan_image3.py:118 with a concrete fix suggestion (add an assertion/guard), while X explicitly validates that same assumption as safe and misses it entirely as a risk. Y also raises a specific test-verification ask (run the e2e test on the right stack) echoing the spirit of Bounty-hunter's request to run a specific benchmark, whereas X only vaguely asks for 'one-line confirmation' on CI. Both reviews are well-grounded with specific code citations and no obvious fabrication, but Y's findings are more concrete and actionable (file/line + specific fix), giving it higher recall and actionability at similar precision.
